questions.question: Explain the three main components of Cost of Quality with examples for each.
keyPoints: Prevention costs - training, process improvement, quality planning
Appraisal costs - testing, inspection, evaluation
Failure costs - rework, repair, customer support

questions.question: Differentiate between Verification and Validation with examples.
keyPoints: Verification: checking if built correctly (reviews, testing against specs)
Validation: checking if built right product (user acceptance testing)
Example: Verification confirms calculator adds 2+2=4; Validation confirms users can use calculator effectively

questions.question: List and briefly explain the six ISO 9126 quality characteristics.
keyPoints: Functionality - features meet requirements
Reliability - performs consistently
Usability - easy to learn/use
Efficiency - resources utilized
Maintainability - easy to modify
Portability - can be transferred to different environments

questions.question: Explain the Defect Life Cycle with all its states.
keyPoints: New - defect reported and registered
Assigned - allocated to developer
Open - being worked on
Fixed - solution implemented
Verified - tested and confirmed
Closed - resolved. May include: Rejected, Deferred, Reopened

questions.question: What is the difference between Quality Assurance and Quality Control?
keyPoints: QA is process-oriented - prevents defects through process improvement
QC is product-oriented - detects and fixes defects through testing
QA is proactive; QC is reactive
Example: QA designs coding standards; QC reviews code against standards

questions.question: Explain the five levels of CMMI maturity model.
keyPoints: Level 1 Initial - ad hoc, chaotic
Level 2 Repeatable - basic processes exist
Level 3 Defined - processes well defined and documented
Level 4 Quantitatively Managed - processes measured and controlled
Level 5 Optimizing - continuous process improvement

questions.question: Define software reliability and maintainability. How are they measured?
keyPoints: Reliability - probability of failure-free operation; measured by MTBF
Maintainability - ease of repair; measured by MTTR
Higher MTBF = better reliability; Lower MTTR = better maintainability

questions.question: Explain McCall's Quality Model. Discuss at least 8 quality factors with their relationships.
keyPoints: McCall's model has 11 quality factors categorized into Product Operation, Product Revision, Product Transition
Product Operation: Correctness, Reliability, Efficiency, Integrity, Usability
Product Revision: Maintainability, Testability, Flexibility
Product Transition: Portability, Reusability, Interoperability
Explain each factor briefly with examples

questions.question: Discuss the importance of software quality in detail. Explain how poor software quality affects organizations and users.
keyPoints: Introduction to software quality importance
Impact on organizations: financial losses, reputational damage, increased maintenance costs
Impact on users: safety risks, productivity loss, data breaches
Examples of software failures (healthcare, aerospace, finance)
Need for quality in critical systems
Conclusion on why quality matters

questions.question: Explain various software testing types and their role in ensuring quality.
keyPoints: Levels: Unit, Integration, System, Acceptance testing
Types: White box, Black box, Static, Dynamic testing
Test coverage: Statement, Branch, Path coverage
Role in quality: Early defect detection, verification/validation, risk mitigation
Examples of each type

questions.question: What are software quality metrics? Discuss at least 5 important metrics used in industry.
keyPoints: Definition and purpose of metrics
Defect density (defects/KLOC)
Code coverage (statement/branch)
Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF)
Mean Time To Repair (MTTR)
Defect Removal Efficiency (DRE)
How to use metrics for quality improvement
